qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Memory-backed late-error cleanup overflows the stack after many completed multipart uploads
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding identifies the same vulnerable code and root cause as the advisory: `abortWithError()` cleans up accumulated uploads through recursive `lib/remove-uploaded-files.js`, and synchronous `storage/memory.js` removal overflows the JavaScript call stack on malformed multipart input. The upstream fix replaces the direct recursive `handleFile(idx + 1)` call with `setImmediate(...)` in that exact file.

selected_finding.title: Memory-backed late-error cleanup overflows the stack after many completed multipart uploads

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding matches the advisory's patched recursion bug in `lib/remove-uploaded-files.js`, and a fresh PoC run from a copied workspace under `.judge` reproduced the advisory-described denial of service: the memory-backed `multer().any()` path hit an uncaught `RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded`, while the disk-backed control path returned the expected `MISSING_FIELD_NAME` error without leftover files.
evidence: The advisory patch `7e66481f8b2e6c54b982b34c152479e096ce2752` changes only `lib/remove-uploaded-files.js`, replacing recursive `handleFile(idx + 1)` with `setImmediate(function () { handleFile(idx + 1) })`, which directly addresses uncontrolled recursion during cleanup.

In the vulnerable 2.1.0 source, `lib/make-middleware.js` calls `removeUploadedFiles(uploadedFiles, remove, ...)` from `abortWithError()`, `lib/remove-uploaded-files.js` recursively walks `uploadedFiles`, and `storage/memory.js` invokes `_removeFile` callbacks synchronously.

The fresh judge-run log at `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_e0583a49/.judge/poc_runs/memory-cleanup-stack-overflow-dos/run.log` shows `memoryProof.fileCount` 3000 with uncaught `RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded`, while `diskControl.result.callback.code` is `MISSING_FIELD_NAME` and `remainingFiles` is `0`.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC cloned Multer 2.1.0 and reproduced the advisory impact: the memory-backed `multer().any()` path crashed with uncaught `RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded` at `memoryProof.fileCount` 3000, while the disk-backed control path returned `MISSING_FIELD_NAME` and left `remainingFiles` at 0.
